title: Starfish and Sea Stacks
summary:
We said goodbye to California and arrived to at Harris Beach State Park, next to Brookings, Oregon. The afternoon was sunny so after checking in we walked down to the beach where I read and people watched, while Colin went on a little run. Abby cooked some delicious shrimp pasta and we ended the day walking up a steep bluff to watch the sunset.

The next day was rainy and cloudy but Colin and I took a morning walk on the beach while Maya zoomed on the sand. We found some very cool rocks covered in mussels and star fish! I have never seen so many starfish in my life and I couldn't stop exclaiming. It rained most of the day, so we set up the projector and had a movie day, complete with popcorn.

We packed up early the next morning since I had a few spots I wanted to see on our way up the coast. The first was a quick viewpoint of some natural bridges over the ocean. The path down to them was closed so we continued on our way to Bandon where we had heard about some sand circle labyrinths that people create. A few days a month, people spend the early morning using rakes to create patterns in the sand that later get erased as the tide comes in. We made it in time to see them and walked through the labyrinth, much to Maya's confusion.

-Helen
